translated: Mei-chan, you've really become someone we can count on lately.
Yes.
It seems my sister also places a great deal of trust in Mei-senpai.
N-Natsumi does?
It sure doesn't look that way to me.
Yeah, I agree.
I feel like Mei-chan naturally leads the 1st and 2nd years and pushes them forward.
Just as Kanon-senpai said, Mei-senpai has been taking the lead in keeping everyone together.
Well, I mean,
if I don't do it, everyone just does whatever they want, so I have no choice.
It's impressive that you can step up like that without hesitating.
If it were me, I'd just leave the leadership stuff to Chi-chan or Ren-chan.
Mei-senpai was also very reliable when we were heading to the Music World.
Oh, I heard about that from Margarete-chan.
You worked really hard, Mei-chan.
Back then, I just thought I'd try to be a bit more like Chisato-senpai.
Well,
looking back, I think I was probably trying a little too hard.
I think that is perfectly fine.
Seeing Mei-senpai work hard has the benefit of making Liella more positive.
I totally get that!
It makes us feel like we can't afford to lose either.
Huh?
You and the others, Kanon-senpai?
No, no, no way.
There's no way you upperclassmen would lose to someone like me.
Don't say that, Mei-chan.
You just said "someone like me," didn't you?
You have more power to move those around you than you realize.
And you have the kindness to support others, too.
Indeed.
Margarete says it often as well.
"I can't let myself be outdone by Mei-senpai."
She says.
Yeah, that sounds like something she'd say.
By the way, your Margarete impression is spot on, Tomari.
I am flattered.
Anyway, Mei-chan, you're the driving force of Liella, so we're counting on you.
The driving force?
Man, that feels like a bit of an exaggeration.
Well, if it's just about lighting a fire under the engine, I can do that anytime.
That is very reassuring.
In that case, please go ahead and light a fire under my sister's motivation for the next exams.
Oh,
that sounds nice.
Then light a fire under my sweets-making too.
Don't get carried away, you two!
I can't look after you that much!
